source.definition: to perform a task or activity to achieve a goal or earn money

explanation.sourceText: The English verb "work" originates from the Old English "weorc" or "wyrcan", meaning "to do, make, or perform labor". It has roots in Proto-Germanic and has been used historically to describe physical or mental effort to accomplish something.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „work“ provine din engleza veche „weorc“ sau „wyrcan“, care înseamnă „a face, a realiza sau a depune muncă“. Are rădăcini în proto-germanică și a fost folosit istoric pentru a descrie efort fizic sau mental pentru a realiza ceva.

source.additional: Used for all kinds of professional or manual activities.

target.definition: a desfășura o activitate pentru a obține sau a câștiga ceva

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian verb "a lucra" derives from the Latin "laborare", meaning "to work" or "to exert effort", referring to physical or mental activity.
explanation.targetText: Verbul românesc „a lucra“ derivă din latină, din „laborare“, care înseamnă „a munci“ sau „a depune efort“, referindu-se la activități fizice sau mentale.

target.additional: Often used in professional contexts as well as everyday tasks, also combined with other nouns.

examples.sourceText: I am working on a project.
He is working on new ideas.
Are you working on the report?
She was working on her goals.
I would like to work on a project.
examples.targetText: Aș dori să lucrez la un proiect.

learningTips.text: Remember the Romanian verb "a lucra" sounds like "look" + "rah" — imagine looking carefully while working hard!
Think of "a lucra" related to the English word "labor" which means work or effort, helping you connect the meanings.
Use the conjugation "Eu lucrez" (I work) and "Am lucrat" (I worked) to practice and remember the verb forms.
Associate "a lucra" with the English word "luck" — working hard can improve your luck and success!
